answerText: Single-threaded but non-blocking. Event loop handles callbacks. Phases: timers, pending callbacks, idle/prepare, poll (I/O), check, close. Microtasks (promises) run between phases. libuv library manages the loop.

Streams process data in chunks rather than loading entirely. Types: readable, writable, duplex, transform. Benefits: memory efficient, composable. Examples: file reading, HTTP requests/responses, compression.

Middleware functions process requests/responses. Called in order. Can modify req/res, end request, or pass to next middleware. Signature: (req, res, next) => {}. Used for authentication, logging, error handling.

questionText: Implement an async/await based route handler.
answerText: Wrap route in async function, use await for promises. Wrap in try/catch for error handling. Pass errors to next() for Express error handling.

questionText: What is the difference between process.nextTick and setImmediate?
answerText: nextTick: microtask, executed after current operation completes. setImmediate: macrotask, executed in next event loop iteration. nextTick higher priority. Generally use setImmediate for heavy work.
